notes: High-chaos seller/trader. Good source of cheap bad decisions.
Mostly harmless junk economy. Will accept humiliating offers if they include cash.
Dangerous buyer/trader. Refusing or insulting him should wake up thug risk.
Trade-focused customer with worse math. Great for risk and weird inventory.
Cash-starved seller. Accepts humiliating lowballs with impressive speed.
Mostly sells sketchy electronics. Panics when asked follow-up questions.
Good buyer for collectible inventory. Harder to fool, sadly literate.
Trades around tools and repairables. Suspicious of cursed appliances, which is unfair but correct.
High-risk source of hot goods. Makes the cops itchy.
Buys cheap inventory and argues like every dollar has a lawyer.
Dangerous trade-heavy NPC. Refusing him badly should wake up thug risk.
Bait buyer. Selling hot goods to this guy should be extremely stupid.
Dispute/return customer. Useful for scam-risk consequences more than normal deals.
Trade goblin. May produce profit or a box that sounds wet.
Trade-heavy old-school hustler. Values presentation more than provenance.
Aggressive buyer and trader. Insults should raise thug risk.
Cash-starved but mostly honest. Sells personal valuables to feed the slots.
